plan.name: azt_collabd
plan.summary: Extract collaboration logic from azt_recorder into a local sync server (azt_collabd) plus a thin client library (azt_collab_client) used by every app in the A-Z+T suite. The server owns credentials, project storage, language-code selection, git operations, and network sync. Clients write files into a working tree (or stream through the server) and call the server to commit and sync. Auto-commit and auto-sync run on every relevant state change, matching the recorder's current behavior.

goals.goal: One sync service per device, shared by all AZT suite apps.
Clients are thin: no dulwich, no git, no credentials, no langcode picker.
Server owns: credentials, project storage, langcode/template selection, git ops, push/pull scheduling, merge handling.
Auto-commit and auto-sync fire on every client state change (not timer-based).
Same public client API regardless of transport (ContentProvider on Android, loopback HTTP on desktop).

non_goals.non_goal: True background daemon / foreground service with periodic timers.
Client-side scratch store or offline buffer (working tree is the only durable store).
Multi-contributor switching UI in v1 (one contributor name per device).

package.name: azt_collabd
package.module: Entrypoint: desktop daemon or Android in-process host.
Device flow, token refresh, GitHub App install checks, GitLab PAT.
dulwich: init, clone, pull, fetch, commit, push; per-repo flock.
LIFT-aware three-way merge driver (guid + field keyed).
Debounced job queue keyed by langcode; connectivity watcher.
Kivy UI: host toggle, GitHub connect, GitLab PAT form, projects screen, language picker (Start New only), per-project status screen.

package.name: azt_collab_client
package.module: Transport discovery: ContentProvider probe → loopback HTTP fallback → spawn server in-process. Retry on SERVICE_RESTARTED.

android.discovery: Probe PackageManager.queryContentProviders for known AZT suite authorities; call ping() via ContentResolver.call to wake the hosting app's provider process.
android.auth: OS-level UID check (provider exported to suite apps only). No shared-secret token needed.
android.files: File I/O via ContentResolver.openFileDescriptor (clients do not need shared storage permission).
android.streaming: Long-running job progress via openFileDescriptor + pipe.

fallback.when: Desktop; Android if no sibling suite app answers provider probe; provider crashed.
fallback.discovery: $AZT_HOME/server.json holds {port, token, pid, version}. Stale file triggers re-spawn.

transport.bundling: Every client APK bundles azt_collabd code (~100-150 KB incremental over dulwich+certifi+urllib3 already shipped). Whichever client starts first and wins the flock on $AZT_HOME/server.lock hosts the server; others discover and connect.

path.value: server-APK private dir, or user-picked DocumentFile

storage.file: {port, token, pid, version} — loopback endpoint hint.
Host prefs, debounce_ms, merge_retry_max, contributor name.
gh_* and gl_* tokens; chmod 600 on POSIX.
Index of langcode → {remote_url, path, created, last_sync}.
storage.dir: last_sync, last_pull_sha, pending_push flag, crash history.

access.desktop: Clients receive Project.working_dir and write LIFT XML + audio directly.
access.android: Clients stream writes through the ContentProvider; server mediates every file op.

locking.edit_lock: Short, per-file. Server issues a lease on (langcode, file_path). Prevents two clients from racing the same file.
locking.commit_lock: Coarse, per-repo flock. Held during git add + commit + fetch + merge + push. Clients attempting edits during this window get BUSY and retry.

data_model.rationale: One-device editing means distributed-commit (per-client git repos) would produce constant three-way merges on the same LIFT file. Shared working tree + central committer avoids that. Clients never run dulwich.

server_rpc_surface.note: Same methods, two wire formats: HTTP/JSON on loopback, Bundle on ContentProvider.call.

keeps.item: Sync-status indicator in the recorder top bar (reads project_status on timer / after each sync event).
Manual "Sync now" button → request_sync + toast on result.
"Start new wordlist" / "Clone URL" entrypoints — dispatch to server UI or call server RPC with progress overlay.
Auto-sync triggers on go_welcome / go_config / go_collab / after recording / after LIFT edit (same triggers as today's _auto_commit_sync / commit_audio_and_sync).
Per-app prefs: theme, ui_language, last_lift (becomes (langcode,) reference), collab_name (read from server, displayed read-only).

deletes.item: CollabScreen class (~500 LOC) and its KV.
Direct dulwich imports anywhere in main.py.
Host-toggle state, GitLab PAT form, GitHub device-flow UI, langcode input (all move into server UI).
LangPickerScreen — moves into server UI behind "Start New".
Prefs keys: collab_host, collab_username, collab_token, collab_langcode, gh_* (all), gl_* (all).

trigger_points.trigger: Any other mutating flow currently calling _auto_commit_sync or commit_audio_and_sync.
Offline → online transition (server-side connectivity watcher drains pending_push flags).

debounce.mode: Trailing-edge debounce of both commit and push within debounce window.
debounce.semantics: request_sync schedules a job debounce_ms in the future; subsequent calls within the window reset the timer. All files written before the timer fires land in one commit and one push.
debounce.rationale: Single user actions (record audio = .wav + .lift update; pick image = image + .lift update) fire multiple request_sync calls. Without debouncing that's N commits + N push cycles per user-visible action. 500 ms groups same-action writes into one commit.

auto_sync_model.offline: Server commits locally, sets state/<code>.json pending_push=True, returns LOCAL_ONLY. No retry loop while offline. On online transition, connectivity watcher iterates projects with pending_push=True and runs one sync each.
auto_sync_model.no_periodic_polling: When online and no pending pushes, server does not poll. Network activity only on client trigger or offline→online edge.

merge_model.scope: All merging is server-side. Clients never see conflicts directly — they see OK or CONFLICTS(paths=[...]).

algorithm.step: Fetch only (updates refs/remotes/origin/<branch>, no working tree change).
Fast-forward if local is ancestor of remote; done.
Remote has nothing new; push local; done.

Diverged → classify paths:
Distinct filenames per entry/contributor. Union merge, always clean. Three-way merge via dulwich porcelain.merge. Use lift_merge driver (guid + field keyed, not line-based). Surface as CONFLICTS.
Commit the merge with merge commit format (see below).
Push. If rejected (race), loop back to step 1 up to merge_retry_max times.

retry.override: request_sync RPC accepts optional merge_retry_max param.
retry.on_exhaustion: Return RACE status; leave local commits in working tree; user can trigger manual sync later.

lift_merge_rules.rule: Parse base, ours, theirs as LIFT trees keyed by entry guid (and sense-id within entry).
Entry present only in ours or only in theirs → keep.
Field changed only on one side → take that side.
Field changed to the same value on both sides → take it.
List-valued fields (senses, examples, audio forms) → union by inner id.
Audio href set on one side, unset on base → take it.
Audio href set to different files on both sides → keep both as additional <form> entries with contributor suffix.

conflict_surface.case: Same field set to two different non-empty values on both sides.
Entry deleted on one side, modified on the other.

conflict_marker.placement: Inside the conflicted entry/field; both versions preserved side-by-side.
conflict_marker.resolution: Normal follow-up edit by user in the recorder UI; recorder draws conflict banner on flagged entries.

merge_commit_format.author: A-Z+T Collab[bot] <azt-collab[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
merge_commit_format.message_template:
Merge origin/<branch> into <branch>
Local commits: <sha> <subject> (<contributor>) ... Remote commits: <sha> <subject> (<author>) ... Conflicts (azt-lift-conflict markers added): <lift-path>: <guid> <field> ...
Co-authored-by: <Local Contributor> <local@device> Co-authored-by: <Remote Author> <remote@email>

notes.note: Conflicts section omitted when merge is clean.

pull_while_editing.rule: Pull runs under commit_lock; edits serialize behind it.
If pull changes files a client has open, server emits PROJECT_UPDATED(langcode, changed_files) event on the transport stream channel.
Recorder reuses existing _reload_and_restore path to refresh UI; prompts user if the open entry is among changed_files.

client_startup.step: Fall back to spawning azt_collabd (loopback + flock).
On persistent failure: blocking dialog "Sync service could not start. The app cannot continue. [Retry] [Quit]".

mid_session_crash.step: Transport detects dropped connection (RPC fails / provider gone / loopback refused).
Boot-time sync does not re-run; just reconnect.
In-flight RPC returns SERVICE_RESTARTED; client retries once.
If reconnect fails after 3 attempts: same blocking dialog as startup.

crash_handling.no_client_scratch: Clients do not buffer writes locally. Working tree is the only durable store. During the ~5 s restart window, writes return BUSY/SERVICE_RESTARTED and the client retries; no dual-source-of-truth reconciliation.
crash_handling.telemetry: Server writes state/crash.log on crash+restart; surfaces in server settings UI ("Last crash: <timestamp> — <reason>").

credentials_and_identity.network_account: One GitHub device-flow token + one GitLab PAT per suite install. Server holds the set; all projects use them. No per-app credentials.
credentials_and_identity.contributor_name: Device-level, set once in server UI. Read by clients via get_contributor() and shown read-only in their UIs. Used in git author field, LIFT <form> attribution, audio filenames.
credentials_and_identity.switch_contributor: Deferred. Add a control in server UI later if needed; no schema change required (already per-commit).

langcode_and_template.picker: Move recorder's LangPickerScreen into server UI.

other_entrypoints.entry: clone_project(url) — derives langcode from repo name or .lift file; no picker.

langcode_and_template.rationale: Any preexisting .lift file already carries its langcode. Only "Start New" needs the picker + template choice.

migration_steps.step: Create azt_collabd/ and azt_collab_client/ packages. Copy collab.py functions verbatim into azt_collabd submodules. collab.py remains as a shim re-exporting from the new package. Recorder untouched. Verify build + one end-to-end sync.
Add stdlib http.server front-end in azt_collabd. Trivial azt_collab_client that calls it. Route one op (do_sync) end-to-end through client. Verify with server launched manually.
Remove Clock import from run_async; callers wrap result. Move or delete run_async from backend.
Replace every _tr() in backend with Status(code, params). Update recorder call sites to translate at UI layer.
Replace from appinfo import APP_SLUG with configure() call made once at startup.
Introduce azt_collabd.store.Credentials. One-shot migration: read legacy keys from prefs.json, copy to credentials.json, strip from prefs. Keep UI-only keys in recorder prefs.
Introduce Project handle + project store. One-time migration moves existing projects from user_data_dir/projects/<code> into $AZT_HOME/projects/<code>. Recorder's last_lift becomes (langcode,).
Add flock around staging, commit, pull, fetch, push, clone, init. BUSY return on timeout.
Add ContentProvider transport on Android; client discovery prefers provider, falls back to loopback. Bundle server code in each client APK with flock-based single-instance guard.
Build server's own Kivy UI reusing CollabScreen + LangPickerScreen widgets. Recorder deletes its CollabScreen.
Add debounced job queue (debounce_ms default 500) and connectivity watcher (drain pending_push on online transition).
Implement lift_merge.py with guid+field keyed three-way merge and azt-lift-conflict markers. Integrate into sync loop.
Implement merge commit message template with local/remote commit lists + co-author trailers.
Implement SERVICE_RESTARTED detection in client rpc.py; spawn retry logic; blocking dialog on persistent failure. crash.log on server side.
Minimal example in docs showing another suite app registering itself, reading a project, committing.

open_questions.question: Separate APK for azt_collabd vs. every client APK bundling the server code? Current decision: bundle in each APK, single-instance via flock + ContentProvider probe.
If ContentProvider route covers all file I/O, we can skip MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE. Confirm all writes go through provider on Android.
For ContentProvider to be accessed cross-APK, all AZT suite apps should either share signing or export the provider with a suite-specific permission. Lock this down before step 9.

decisions_locked_in.decision: Merge retry default: 3; configurable via sync.merge_retry_max; per-call override on request_sync.
Merge commit author/committer: A-Z+T Collab[bot]; Co-authored-by trailers for every distinct author on both sides; conflicts section only when present.
Auto-sync: trigger-based (every state change + offline→online edge), not timer-based.
Debounce: 500 ms default, both commit and push; sync.debounce_ms configurable.
Language-code and template selection live in server UI, Start New flow only.
One network account + one contributor name per device; switch-contributor deferred.
No client-side scratch store; blocking dialog if server can't start.
Every client APK bundles the server; flock + ContentProvider probe for single-instance.
Clients write text/files; server commits. (Not: clients commit and share commits.)
Transport: ContentProvider preferred on Android; loopback HTTP+JSON fallback on desktop / when no provider answers.
